intake_object: Corroboration-first lead intake row, not a seeded case row
minimum_metadata_fields: lead_id; discovered_from_public_url; access_date_utc; language; jurisdiction; source_surface_type; summary_of_claimed_event; public_artifact_type; rights_or_terms_note; translation_status; privacy_safety_screen
mandatory_upgrade_fields_before_seeded_u: At least one higher-authority corroboration target recorded with route plan, plus one exact public anchor that can be cited or re-fetched without private access; preserve whether the lead is still screenshot-only, thread-only, or issue-only
exclusion_rules: Reject private messages, credentials, harassment material, doxxing, unverifiable screenshots without public anchor, and leads whose only support is a reposted claim with no recoverable public origin
what_counts_as_real_progress_now: A machine-readable lead schema with mandatory corroboration and safety fields, or one existing lead upgraded into a public-authority / public-record / direct-document route while preserving the original low-authority lead status as provenance
what_does_not_count_as_progress_now: Collecting more uncoded links, copying social posts into case prose, or treating thread visibility as incident truth or as sufficient for seeded atlas inclusion
claim_ceiling_before_upgrade: Lead queue only; discovery and route-design value, no empirical or legal claim support
dashboard_or_table_hook: Lead queue; corroboration backlog; source-upgrade funnel; Figure 1 planned-frontier tooltip
paper_thesis_use: Lets LIMEN defend why obscure web-lead material is visible in the observatory architecture without letting it contaminate headline evidence tiers, case counts, or legal interpretation; useful for methods sections, reviewer-response, and thesis chapters on observability bias
next_smallest_publishability_move: Create or promote exactly one lead-intake schema/queue artifact before adding any deliberate sf14 family expansion, then freeze the family below seeded use until a higher-authority corroboration route exists

intake_object: Residual-frontier novelty note or bounded queue row, not a seeded incidence claim
minimum_metadata_fields: frontier_id; public_url; access_date_utc; language; jurisdiction; anomaly_type; why_current_taxonomy_is_stressed; public_artifact_type; authority_band; translation_status; dignity_and_sensationalism_screen
mandatory_upgrade_fields_before_seeded_u: At least one explicit inclusion rule, one exclusion rule, one authority-band label, and one statement of what stronger direct source would be needed before the row could affect seeded taxonomy or manuscript main-text claims
exclusion_rules: Reject humiliation bait, satire without clear clarification context, aesthetic-only curiosities, unverifiable reposts, and any row whose only value is sensational novelty without methods or taxonomy consequence
what_counts_as_real_progress_now: A residual-category intake note with exclusion rules and evidentiary thresholds, or one existing novelty-boundary row reclassified into an already-supported category because the frontier test showed the current taxonomy was sufficient
what_does_not_count_as_progress_now: Adding many colorful anecdotes, widening residual counts, or using novelty/absurdity examples as if they proved prevalence, legal status, or a new top-level taxonomy by themselves
claim_ceiling_before_upgrade: Residual-category queue only; bounded novelty/taxonomy stress test, not count-bearing evidence
dashboard_or_table_hook: Residual-category queue; novelty-authority matrix; taxonomy stress-test panel; Figure 2 residual-methods note
paper_thesis_use: Gives the first LIMEN paper and thesis a reviewer-legible explanation for how novelty-boundary material can improve differentiation and taxonomy testing without silently entering incident denominators or sensationalizing edge cases
next_smallest_publishability_move: Record one explicit residual/frontier intake rule bundle before any family growth, then use it only to test whether a candidate belongs in an existing category, a queue, or a future subtype proposal
